links.targetTitle: The Swarm push cycle serializes, AES-256-GCM encrypts, uploads to /bytes, then writes a feed SOC.
The Swarm pull cycle polls user and document manifests then decrypts batches into the inbox.
Each reactor IChannel exposes three mailboxes: inbox, outbox, and deadLetter.
Powerhouse documents are stored as an append-only operation log, not state snapshots
Each document has a feed at ph:v2:doc:<id> pointing to a manifest that indexes operation batches on /bytes
A user manifest indexes drives and a drive manifest lists documents plus folder structure
SwarmChannel implements IChannel so the reactor SyncManager is transport-agnostic
Each reactor IChannel exposes three mailboxes: inbox, outbox, and deadLetter.
Swarm manifests form a three-tier hierarchy of user, drive, and document feeds keyed by deterministic keccak256 topics.

global.title: SwarmChannel push batches ordinals into SyncOperations; pull walks user → drive → doc manifests to rebuild inboxes
global.content:
SwarmChannel implements two directional flows over the reactor's SyncManager.

**Push (outbox)**: SyncManager detects new ordinals, groups ops into SyncOperations, and hands them to SwarmChannel. The channel serializes, encrypts, uploads to `/bytes`, writes the reference to the per-document feed, and updates drive/user manifests via `ManifestManager`.

**Pull (inbox)**: SwarmChannel polls the user manifest at `ph:v2:user:<address>` to discover drives, reads each drive manifest to enumerate documents, reads document manifests to find operation batches, downloads batches from `/bytes` (auto-decrypted via SWE prefix detection), creates SyncOperations, and adds them to the inbox. SyncManager then applies via `reactor.load(docId, branch, ops)`.

Supporting state is durable: `sync_cursors` in PGlite tracks ordinal progress and survives page reload, and failed operations land in `sync_dead_letters` for persisted, queryable retries. No custom persistence is needed.
global.description: Push encrypts and uploads to /bytes then updates manifests via ManifestManager; pull polls the user manifest, reads drive and doc manifests, downloads batches, and applies via reactor.load().
